name: ITPS206 Midterm Topic 1 - Introduction to Computer Networking

questions.questionText: History of Long-Distance Communication? (7 Items)
questions.answer: Smoke Signals, Pigeon Courier, Semaphore Flags, Pony Express, Electrical Telegraph, Wireless Telegraphy, Telephone

questions.questionText: Who invented Semaphore Flags?
Who invented Electrical Telegraph?
Who invented Telephone?
Types of data that represents via sine wave.
Types of data that represents via square wave.
Analog data is represented via?
Digital Data is represented via?
Who coined Information Theory?
The one where the information came from.
It encodes the source's information.
Picks signal and send it.
The path the message will traverse.
Filters and receives message.
Decodes the receiver's message.
Destination of the message.
Source or destination of a message. If the data starts or stops there, it's a _________________.
These connects the end devices to the network or connect multiple networks together. They ensure data flows efficiently across the infrastructure.
Gets the Internet, a.k.a, the ISP.
A device that connects devices to the internet.
The ROAD or Channel over which the message travels.
Rules that software follow that makes communication possible.
Components of a Network? (4 Items)
questions.answer: End Devices, Intermediary Devices, Network Media, Network Services and Protocols

questions.questionText: What does IEEE mean?
questions.answer: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers

questions.questionText: Who decides ethernet cable needs specific radio waves, volts, and so on? (Acronym only)
What does IETF mean?
An open international community of network designers, operators, and researchers? (Acronym only)
These are the official documents of the IETF that define every protocol on the internet, from TCP/IP and HTTP to SMTP. (Acronym only)
What does RFC mean?
A global federation of national standard bodies. (Acronym only)
What does ISO mean?
OSI Model from top to bottom. (7 Items)
questions.answer: Application, Presentation, Session, Transport, Network, Data-Link, Physical

questions.questionText: This organization focuses on global telecommunications. (Acronym only)
What does ITU mean?
2 organization that focuses on physical side of networking.
What does TIA mean?
What does EIA mean?
5 Network Orgranizations? (Acronyms only)
Why standards matter? (3 items)
IEEE Standard for 802 family, version only. (3 Itmes)
IEEE Standard 802 for Ethernet (Wired).
IEEE Standard 802 for Wi-Fi (Wireless).
IEEE Standard 802 for Bluetooth & WPAN (Wireless Personal Area Network).
What does WAP mean?
The transmission of voice and data without cable or wires.

name: ITPS206 Midterm Topic 2 - Network Types and Topologies

questions.questionText: Types of Computer Network that is usually owned by individual and very limited range of 10 meters.
Types of Computer Network that uses wireless or wired connection within small Geographic Area, usually within the room.
Types of Computer Network that is composed of multiple LANs connections.
Types of Computer Network within the city only.
Types of Computer Network around the world.
Types of Common Computer Network. (5 Items, acronym only)
Types of Computer Network acts as an interface for the storage and the user.
Types of Computer Network for protection by hiding addresses, IPs, and so on...
Types of Computer Network that is wireless version of wired network for small area only.
Types of Specialized Computer Network? (3 Items acronym only)
A Network Architecture where there is a centralized node where other devices connect.
A Network Architecture that directly connects devices to each other.
2 Types of Network Architecture.
Type of Network Topology of what the real-world layout.
Type of Network Topology of how data flows from one device to another.
2 Types of Network Topologies.
Network Topology that directly connects 2 nodes or devices.
Network Topology that has a speaker and listener.
Network Topology that has a master and slaves.
Network Topology where it needs to go from a middle node/s to go to the final node.
Network Topology that is closed loop version of Bus Topology.
Network Topology that is hierarchical. A combination of star and bus.
Network Topology where all notes connect to a central hub/switch.
Network Topology where every node is connected to every node.
Network Topologies, Not the Types (Physical and Logical). (8 Items without the word TOPOLOGY).
questions.answer: Point-To-Point, Point-To-Multipoint, Multidrop, Bus, Ring, Tree, Star, Mesh

name: ITPS206 Midterm Topic 3 - Network Model and Protocols

questions.questionText: A conceptual framework that describes how data moves from an application on one computer, through a network medium, to an application on another computer.
Definition of OSI acronym.
OSI Model Layer that is the interface between the user and the network. It's where you interact with applications like Chrome, Outlook, or Skype.
OSI Model Layer that presents data to the application layer. It handles data translation, encryption (for security), and compression.
7 Open System Intercommunication layers.
questions.answer: Application, Presentation, Session, Transport, Network, Data-Link, Physical

questions.questionText: OSI Model Layer that manages the dialogue between two computers. It starts, maintains, and ends the communication session.
OSI Model Layer that ensures the data is delivered correctly. It handles Error Recovery and Flow Control (making sure the sender doesn't send data faster than the receiver can handle).
Transport Layer that is fast.
Transport Layer that is accurate and reliable.
OSI Model Layer that handles routing. It finds the best path for data to travel across different networks using logical addresses (IP).
A packet carries a __________.
A process of dividing the messages or data into smaller fragments.
OSI Model Layer that handles communication between devices on the same local network. It uses MAC addresses (hardcoded on your network card).
The difference between Switch and Hub is that Hub ___________ to all devices while Switch only do it once then it doesn't need to do it again.
OSI Model Layer that is the actual hardware. It involves cables, radio waves (Wi-Fi), and electrical signals (bits: 1s and 0S).
4 Layers of TCP/IP model.
Common Network Protocols (OSI Model) for Application Layer.
Common Network Protocols (OSI Model) for Presentation Layer.
Common Network Protocols (OSI Model) for Session Layer.
Common Network Protocols (OSI Model) for Transport Layer.
Common Network Protocols (OSI Model) for Network Layer.
Common Network Protocols (OSI Model) for Data Link Layer.
Governs how web browsers request and receive web pages.
Resolves human-readable names into IP addresses.
The standard for sending electronic mail.
Used for the exchange of files between a client and a server.
Manages encryption and decryption for secure data transfer.
Standards for image formatting and compression
Standards for video and audio compression and coding.
Allows applications on separate computers to communicate over a LAN.
Allows a program to cause a subroutine to execute in another address space (commonly on another computer).
A method for implementing virtual private networks.
A connection-oriented protocol that ensures data arrives in order and without errors through "acknowledgments."
A connectionless protocol used for speed. It does not check for errors or arrival (used in VoIP and gaming).
The primary protocols responsible for logical addressing and routing.
Used by network devices to send error messages (e.g., the requested service is unavailable)
A suite of protocols for securing IP communications by authenticating and encrypting each IP packet.
The most widely used wired LAN technology.
The standard for wireless local area networking.
Resolves a known IP address to a physical MAC address.
Used to establish a direct connection between two nodes (like your router to your ISP).
A short-range wireless technology standard.
Technology used to transmit digital data over telephone lines.
The technical standard for Gigabit Ethernet over copper wiring.

A hardware component, typically a circuit board or chip, that allows a computer to connect to a network. It serves as the middleman between your device and the data cables or wireless signals that carry information.
Key characteristics of NIC.
It takes the digital data from your computer (parallel data) and converts it into electrical, optical, or radio signals (serial data) that can travel over a wire or through the air.
Every NIC has a unique, permanent identification number burned into it at the factory, known as a MAC Address (Media Access Control).
It manages the speed of data. If the network is sending data faster than your computer can process it, the NIC holds that data in a small onboard memory buffer until the CPU is ready.
Types of NIC.
Features an RJ-45 port where you plug in an Ethernet cable. These are known for being very stable and fast.
Uses antenna to receive radio waves. This is the Wi-Fi card found inside almost every modern laptop and smartphone.
An external adapter that plugs into a USB port to give a device network capability (common for modern laptops that are too thin for a standard Ethernet port).
00-00-00-11-11-11. What is the meaning of the 0s?
00-00-00-11-11-11. What is the meaning of the 1s?
A basic networking device used to connect multiple computers or devices together in a Local Area Network (LAN).
Key characteristics of Hub.
questions.answer: Shared Bandwidth, Half-Duplex Communication, Single Collision Domain, Layer 1 Device

questions.questionText: If a hub has a speed of 100 Mbps and 10 computers are talking at once, they all have to share that 100 Mbps. It gets slower as more devices are added.
Devices on a hub cannot talk and listen at the exact same time. It's like a Walkie-Talkie; if two people try to speak at once, a collision occurs, and the data is corrupted.
Because everyone is on the same wire logically, only one person can successfully send data at a time.
In the OSI model, a hub exists only at the Physical Layer. It just moves electrical bits; it doesn't understand MAC addresses or IP addresses.
A high-speed networking device that connects multiple devices (computers, printers, servers) within a Local Area Network.
Key characteristics of Switch.
questions.answer: Full-Duplex Communication, Dedicated Bandwidth, Collision-Free, Layer 2 Device

questions.questionText: Allows devices to send and receive data at the same time. It's like a Telephone rather than a Walkie-Talkie.
If you have a 1 Gbps switch, every single port gets the full 1 Gbps speed. They don't have to share it like they do on a hub.
Because the switch creates a dedicated path between the ports, they never crash into each other.
In the OSI model, a switch operates at the Data Link Layer because it makes decisions based on MAC Address.

A sophisticated networking device that connects different networks together. While a switch connects devices inside a house or office (a LAN), it connects that entire house to the outside world (the Internet/WAN).
Key characteristics of Router.
questions.answer: Layer 3 Device, Boundary Device, Network Address Translation

questions.questionText: The router acts as a GATEAWAY. It is the security guard that sits between your private home network and the scary public internet.
This is a special MAGIC TRICK your home router does. It takes the one IP address your ISP gives you and shares it among your phone, laptop, etc...
A network administrator manually types the paths into the router's configuration.
Routers use protocols (like ESPF or EIGRP) to talk to each other. They GOSSIP about the networks they know.
2 Types of routing.
The physical paths used to carry data.
Guided transmission media are ________.
Unguided transmission media are ______.
Most common for LANs. It uses electrical pulses, pairs of copper wires are twisted together to form a single communication link.
Twisted pair cable is twisted due to __________.
Two main types of Twisted Pair of Cables.
questions.answer: Unshielded Twisted Pair, Shielded Twisted Pair

questions.questionText: This is the standard cable used in almost all homes and offices. It consists of color-coded copper wires with no extra shielding between the wires and the outer plastic jacket.
Pros of Unshielded Twisted Pair.
Cons of Unshielded Twisted Pair.
This version includes a metal foil or braided mesh casing around each pair (or the entire bundle).
Pros of Shielded Twisted Pair cable.
Cons of Shielded Twisted Pair cable.
questions.answer: More Expensive, Thicker, Harder to Bend

questions.questionText: Heavy-duty veteran of the cable world.
Uses pulses of light sent through glass or plastic strands.
Data is converted into electromagnetic waves and sent in all directions.
Your router uses radio waves to send internet data to your phone.
Unlike radio waves that scatter everywhere, this are unidirectional. They travel in a very narrow straight beam.
Essentially a microwave relay station in space. It solves the problem of mountains and the Earth's curvature by moving the tower thousands of miles into the sky.
Uses high-frequency light waves that are just below the visible red-light spectrum. Because these waves have very short wavelengths, they behave more like light than radio waves.
